label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's tackle this question. The passage is about Lao Yang, who wanted to be a writer when he was young but now works in a factory. His daughter needs to write an article titled "My Father," so Lao Yang helps by writing it. He believes his article is good and expects the teacher to like it. However, the teacher says it's off-topic. The question is asking what Lao Yang hoped for. The options are about surprising the teacher, his article being chosen, children liking it, or everyone knowing him. First, let's look at the text. After writing the article, Lao Yang "was sure Miss GAO would like it." Then he asks his daughter if it was chosen to be posted. The key part is "he asked his daughter if the article had been chosen to post to the editorial department." This suggests he hoped the article would be selected by the teacher to be submitted. So his hope was that his article would be chosen, which is option B. Other options don't fit as well. He didn't mention
